title: Bisected graph matching improves automated pairing of bilaterally homologous neurons from connectomes
abstract: Proposes bisected graph matching, a method that improves automated pairing of bilaterally homologous neurons in connectomes. By leveraging known correspondences between brain hemispheres as seeds, the algorithm achieves higher accuracy in matching left-right neuron pairs compared to standard graph matching approaches, validated on the Drosophila larval connectome.
notes: Duplicate of existing. Cross-listed with Vogelstein and Priebe labs.
